content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN and an array AAA of size NNN, A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2,..., A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​.

We define a process as shown below, your task is to find the expected value of ansansans at the end of it.

1)1) 1) Choose a random number xxx, equiprobably in range [1,N][1, N][1,N]. Initialize ans=Ax,l=x,r=x.ans = A_x, l = x, r = x.ans=Ax​,l=x,r=x.

2)2) 2) Repeat the following N−1N - 1N−1 times :


define a temporary variable bbb as follows :

If l=1l = 1l=1, set b=1b = 1b=1;
else if r=nr = nr=n set b=0b = 0b=0;
else set b=0b = 0b=0 or 111 with equal probability.


If b=0b = 0b=0, first set l=l−1l = l - 1l=l−1, then set ans=ans+Al⋅(r−l+1)ans = ans + A_l \cdot (r - l + 1)ans=ans+Al​⋅(r−l+1).
If b=1b = 1b=1, first set r=r+1r = r + 1r=r+1, then set ans=ans+Ar⋅(r−l+1)ans = ans + A_r \cdot (r - l + 1)ans=ans+Ar​⋅(r−l+1).
It can be shown by the end of the process l=1l = 1l=1 and r=Nr = Nr=N.

Print the expected value of ansansans under modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

Formally, it can be proven that the value can be written as PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP​, where P,QP, QP,Q are integers and relatively prime and Qmod  (109+7)≠0Q \mod (10^9 + 7) \ne 0Qmod(109+7)=0. Then, you need to print the unique integer RRR in range [0,109+7)[0, 10^9 + 7)[0,109+7) such that Q⋅R≡PQ \cdot R \equiv PQ⋅R≡P (mod 109+710^9 + 7109+7).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the size of the array
The second line of each test case contains NNN integers - A1,A2,....,ANA_1, A_2, ...., A_NA1​,A2​,....,AN​ representing the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the expected value of the variable ansansans.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first sample, there is only one possible sequence of moves, which yields ansansans = 5.
In the second sample, there are 222 possible sequences of moves:

x=1x = 1x=1 :


ans=A1=1,l=1,r=1ans = A_1 = 1, l = 1, r = 1ans=A1​=1,l=1,r=1
in the first and only step, bbb must be 111 because l=1l = 1l=1.
ans=ans+A2⋅2=5,l=1,r=2ans = ans + A_2 \cdot 2 = 5, l = 1, r = 2ans=ans+A2​⋅2=5,l=1,r=2. The process terminates.


x=2x = 2x=2 : This gives ans=2+2⋅1=4ans = 2 + 2 \cdot 1 = 4ans=2+2⋅1=4.

Since both of these are equiprobable, the expected value of ans=ans = ans= 4+52=92\frac{4 + 5}{2} = \frac{9}{2}24+5​=29​.You can verify 2⋅500000008≡92 \cdot 500000008 \equiv 92⋅500000008≡9 (mod 109+710^9 + 7109+7).
